title: TruthRL: Incentivizing Truthful LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
venue: arXiv:2509.25760v1 [cs.CL] (Meta Reality Labs / UVA / UW / FAIR)
key_claims: Accuracy-driven training (vanilla SFT, binary-reward RL) inherently incentivizes guessing over abstention, amplifying hallucinations.
A simple ternary reward (+1 correct, 0 abstain, -1 hallucinate) with GRPO reduces hallucinations by up to 28.9% and improves truthfulness by up to 21.1% over vanilla RL.
Improvement arises from genuine knowledge boundary recognition, not over-conservatism: TruthRL abstains on questions where the model genuinely lacks knowledge.
More complicated rewards (knowledge-enhanced, reasoning-enhanced) do not outperform the simple ternary reward.
LLM-based verifier is critical; rule-based (string matching) verifier causes model collapse (T=-3.6) due to semantic mismatch.
method: GRPO-based online RL with a ternary reward function. For each question, G responses are sampled; advantage is computed via group-normalized rewards: A_i = (r_i - mean(r)) / std(r). Under ternary reward, abstention (r=0) gets higher advantage than hallucination (r=-1), explicitly incentivizing abstention over guessing. Knowledge boundary probing samples 256 responses per training question to identify OOK questions. Optional knowledge-enhanced variant gives +1 for abstention on OOK questions. Training: VeRL framework, 8xH100, GRPO with lr=1e-6, batch=64, beta=0.001, eps=0.2, max_ctx=16384, max_gen=2048. vLLM rollouts with tensor_parallel=2. Evaluation: LLM-as-a-judge (Llama3.3-70B) with T = Acc - Hall (w1=1,w2=0,w3=1). Benchmarks: CRAG, NQ, HotpotQA, MuSiQue with/without retrieval.

results.baseline: Simple ternary: T=25.6, H=18.8% (knowledge-enhanced slightly worse)

limitations: Single training dataset (CRAG only); generalization to code, math, creative writing untested.
LLM judge dependency: reward signal relies on Llama3.3-70B verifier with inherent biases.
Truthfulness metric sets w2=0 (no credit for appropriate abstention in evaluation).
Knowledge boundary probing requires 256 samples per question (expensive, model-specific).
Reasoning reward integration shows mixed results; mechanism for implicit reasoning improvement unexplored.
No middle-ground between string matching (collapses) and full LLM judge explored.
Abstention classification method during reward assignment not fully detailed.
No comparison with calibration-theoretic methods (RLCR, Rewarding Doubt, CDA).
Binary abstention only ('I don't know') -- no graded confidence expression.
connections: Direct foundation of TruthRL-personalized- project; ternary reward implemented in training/verl/verl/utils/reward_score/truthrl_qa.py.
Table 5 rule-based collapse (T=-3.6) motivates project's hybrid approach: symbolic process reward + LLM outcome reward.
Section 4.6 mixed reasoning reward results validate project Direction 3: target decidable structural properties, not LLM-judged reasoning.
GRPO advantage mechanism (group-normalized) is key context for composing additional reward signals.
Consistent with AbstentionBench finding that reasoning fine-tuning degrades abstention by 24%.
Complementary to RLCR (Brier score) and Rewarding Doubt (log scoring rules) at ICLR 2026.
